content.full_text:
,. 
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Mark Branfitt 
Executive Officer 
December I , 2016 
Sonoma Local Agency Formation Commission 
575 Administration Drive, Rm. l 04A 
Santa Rosa, CA 95403 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-16-222 
Dear Mr. Branfitt: 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Commissioner Richard Holmer 
regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Please note, 
the Fair Political Practices Commission does not provide advice relating to past conduct. This letter 
should not be construed as assistance on any conduct that may have already taken place. (See 
Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).) Additionally, we are only providing advice under the conflict of 
interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as 
common law conflict of interest or Section 1090. 
QUESTION 
May Commissioner Holmer take part in decisions regarding the detachment of parcels from 
the Palm Drive Health Care District when the decisions would decrease the current assessment on 
the Commissioner's property? 
CONCLUSION 
Because the decisions involve the reduction and eventual repeal of an assessment that 
applies to the Commissioner's property, the Commissioner's interest is explicitly involved in the 
decisions and the effect of the decisions on the interest in property is foreseeable and material. 
Accordingly, the Commissioner may not take part in the decisions. 
FACTS 
Richard Holmer, a Director at the Sweetwater Springs Water District, was elected to serve 
on the Sonoma Local Agency Formation Commission as one of two Special District members, on 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code ofRegulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-16-222 
Page No. 2 
July 29th, 2016, by the boards of special districts in the County. Commissioner Holmer is a resident 
and landowner of Forestville, CA. 
At its September 7111, 2016 and October 51\ 2016 meetings, the Sonoma Local Agency 
Fonnation Commission adjudicated an application seeking detachment of parcels within the 
Guemeville, Monte Rio, and Forestville Unified School Districts from the Palm Drive Health Care 
District. The area proposed for detachment includes approximately 12,321 parcels. If approved, the 
detachment application would result in a decrease, and eventual suspension, of a voter approved 
property parcel tax that was instituted in 200 I. Landowners in the detachment area would still be 
required to pay an apportionment of the parcel tax towards debt repayment by the District, until 
the debt is discharged. Upon detachment, the annual assessment would drop from $155 to 
approximately $75 per taxable parcel. Upon discharge of the debt (estimated in the year 2036) the 
annual assessment would drop to $0. 
Commissioner Holmer owns one parcel in the Forestville Unified School District, an area 
proposed for detachment. Commissioner Holmer pays the $155 annual assessment, which would be 
reduced and eventually eliminated if the application were approved. While Commissioner Holmer 
recused himself from consideration of the detachment application at the September 7th and October 
5th Commission meetings, the detachment decisions may be reconsidered in the future. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using 
his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. 
(Section 87103.) A conflict of interest may arise only when the reasonably foreseeable financial 
effect of a governmental decision on a public official's interests is material. Different standards 
apply to determine whether a reasonably foreseeable financial effect on an interest will be material 
depending on the nature of the interest. Interests from which a conflict of interest may arise are 
defined in Section 8 7103. 
Under Sections 82033 and 87103(b ), an official has an interest in any real property owned 
directly, indirectly, or beneficially by the public official or his or her immediate family, if the 
interest has a fair market value of $2,000 or more. The only interest identified, which the decisions 
in question may implicate, is Commissioner Holmer's interest in property within the Forestville 
Unified School District. Accordingly, we must determine whether the financial effect on the 
Commissioner's property resulting from the decisions is foreseeable and material. 
Generally, a financial effect is presumed to be reasonably foreseeable if the interest is 
"explicitly involved" in the governmental decision. An interest is explicitly involved in a decision if 
it is a named party in, or the subject of, a governmental decision before the official or the official's 
agency. (Regulation 18701 (a).) For an interest in real property, the interest is the subject of the 
proceeding and thus "explicitly involved" in the decision anytime the decision "[w]ould impose, 
repeal, or modify any taxes, fees, or assessments that apply to the parcel." (Regulations l 8701(a) 
and 18702.2(a)(3 ).) Likewise, the repeal or modification of an existing tax, fee, or assessment on 
real property is considered a material effect. (Regulation l 8702.2(a)(3).) 
.. 


r 
File No. A-16-222 
Page No. 3 
Because decisions regarding the detachment of parcels from the Palm Drive Health Care 
District involve the repeal or modification of the current assessment on the Commissioner's 
property, his interest is explicitly involved in the decisions and the financial effect of the decisions 
on the interest in the property is both foreseeable and material. Accordingly, the Commissioner may 
not make, participate, or use his official positon to influence the decisions. 
Additionally, you have asked that we specifically consider the applicability of the Act's 
public generally exception. The Act prohibits an official from taking part in a decision only if the 
decisions effect on the official's interest is distinguishable from the effect on the public generally. 
(Section 87103.) Commonly referred to as the "public generally exception," Regulation 18703(a) 
sets forth the general rule: 
A governmental decision's financial effect on a public official's financial interest is 
indistinguishable from its effect on the public generally if the official establishes that 
a significant segment of the public is affected and the effect on his or her financial 
interest is not unique compared to the effect on the significant segment. 
A '"significant segment of the public" consists of at least 25 percent of: (I) the businesses or 
nonprofit entities; (2) the real property, commercial real property, or residential real property; or (3) 
the individuals within the official's jurisdiction. (Regulation 18703(b ). ) 
You have stated that the area proposed for detachment includes approximately 12,321 
parcels. While unlikely the parcels within this area will be uniquely affected by the decision, it does 
not appear that the number of parcels constitute a significant segment of the official's jurisdiction.2 
Absent additional facts, Commissioner Holmer may not invoke the public generally exception. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
BGL:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
v5,;r 
By: 
Brian G. Lau 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division 
2 For purposes of the public generally exception, an official's "jurisdiction" is the geographical area in which 
the official's agency has jurisdiction, the geographical area the official was elected to represent, or the area to which the 
official's authority and duties are limited if not elected. (Section 82035; Regulation l 8703(d).) In this case, 
Commissioner Holmer has not been elected to represent a limited geographical area. Accordingly, the Commissioner's 
jurisdiction for purposes of applying the public generally exception is the jurisdiction of the Sonoma Local Agency 
Formation Commission, which appears 10 be the County of Sonoma.
